Landing.metaTitle: LaunchWake — Ship it. We'll make the waves.
Landing.metaDescription: The distribution co-pilot for technical founders. For every ship, LaunchWake tells you where to post it, how to post without getting banned, and what actually drove signups. Try the free Launch Checker — no login.
Landing.heroEyebrow: For founders who'd rather be coding
Landing.heroLede: LaunchWake is the distribution co-pilot for technical founders. It finds where your users actually hang out, tells you exactly what to post and where — then shows you what really drove signups.
Landing.heroTryHeadline: Try it free — paste a GitHub repo, get a distribution plan. No account.
Landing.problemTitle: You can build anything. Getting users is the hard part.
Landing.problemLead: Most technical founders ship a great product into silence. Not because it's bad — because marketing feels like a foreign language you never wanted to learn.

pains.p: You know Reddit, HN and X exist. You don't know which communities, which angle, or the rules that get you banned instead of upvoted.
Every hour writing a launch post is an hour not building. So marketing slips — and the product stays invisible.
You post, you hope, you never really know which channel brought the signups. So you can't do more of what works.

Landing.howTitle: Analysis and guidance — you stay in control.
Landing.howLead: LaunchWake never auto-posts for you (that's how accounts get banned). It does the thinking; you press publish.

steps.h: Point it at what you're building
steps.p: Drop your product URL, GitHub repo or changelog. LaunchWake understands what you shipped and who it's for.

steps.h: Find where your users actually are
steps.p: It ranks a curated catalog of real communities by fit for your product — with each one's tone, rules and ban risk.

steps.h: Get the post, the place, the timing
steps.p: Platform-native drafts plus where and when to post them — safely, without the spammy patterns that get you nuked. You hit publish.
Tracked links close the loop: this post → these clicks → these signups. Now you know what to double down on.

Landing.whyTitle: Not another AI that posts spam for you.

why.p: It tells you the smart move; you make it. No bot armies, no fake accounts — just the analysis you're missing.
Every recommendation is grounded in a real community's rules, so your posts land instead of getting your account nuked.
Every suggestion is tied to real attribution. Stop guessing which channel worked — watch it.

Landing.toolsTitle: Start getting value before you sign up.
Landing.toolLaunchCheckerDesc: Paste a GitHub repo and get a ranked distribution plan — which channels fit, their ban risk, and how to post in each.
Landing.toolBanRiskDesc: The posting playbook for every community founders launch in — rules, ban risk, and the safe way in. “Can I post my startup on r/SaaS?”
Landing.pricingTitle: Start free. Pay when it makes waves.
Landing.pricingHint: No credit card. Built for indie hackers, dev-tool founders & the teams that launch for them.
Landing.finalTitle: Your next launch deserves an audience.
Landing.finalLead: Ship it. We'll make the waves.

Pricing.metaDescription: Simple pricing for LaunchWake: Free forever for your first launches, Pro at $29/mo for unlimited plans + attribution, and a seat-based Team plan for agencies & DevRel. No credit card to start.
Pricing.title: Start free. Pay when it makes waves.
Pricing.lede: The intelligence is the product — where to post, how to post without getting banned, and what actually drove signups. Try it free; upgrade when distribution becomes part of every ship.
Pricing.hint: No credit card to start · cancel anytime · never auto-posts on your behalf.

faq.q: Is there really a free plan?
faq.a: Yes — Free is forever. You get 1 project and 2 distribution plans a month, with the full where-to-post intelligence, ban-risk rules, and platform-native drafts. No credit card.
A distribution plan is the ranked set of channels we generate for one ship (a release, feature, or post). Free includes 2 per month; Pro and Team are unlimited.
Team is seat-based: $29 per seat with a 3-seat minimum, billed monthly through Stripe. Add or remove seats anytime from the billing portal. Built for agencies and DevRel teams running many products.
Never. LaunchWake generates the plan and the drafts; you press publish from your own account. Auto-posting is how accounts get banned — it's out of scope on purpose.
Yes. Manage or cancel your subscription from the billing portal in Settings; you keep access until the end of the period, then drop to Free.

Pricing.gateTitle: See your own distribution plan first
Pricing.gateBody: Not sure yet? Paste a GitHub repo into the free Launch Checker and get a ranked plan for your product — no account needed.

Channels.metaTitle: Where can I post my startup? Channel rules & ban risk — LaunchWake
Channels.metaDescription: A directory of the communities technical founders launch in — Hacker News, Reddit, Product Hunt and more — with each one's posting rules, ban risk and best time to post.
Channels.title: Where can you post your startup — safely?
Channels.lede: Every community has unwritten rules, and breaking them gets your post removed or your account banned. Here's the posting playbook for the channels technical founders actually launch in.

ChannelDetail.gateTitle: Is {name} even the right channel for your launch?
ChannelDetail.gateBody: Paste your GitHub repo into the free Launch Checker and get a ranked plan — which channels fit your product, their ban risk, and how to post in each. No account needed.

StateOfLaunches.metaTitle: The State of Developer Launches — where dev products actually get signups
StateOfLaunches.metaDescription: An anonymized aggregate of thousands of developer product launches: which channels drive the most signups, which convert best, how it differs by category, where posts get removed, and the best time to post.
StateOfLaunches.ogDescription: Where developer products actually get signups — an anonymized aggregate of real launches, by channel, category, ban risk and timing.
StateOfLaunches.lede: We aggregated where technical founders actually launch — anonymized across every tracked launch — to answer the question no blog post can: which channels really drive signups, which convert, and where posts get removed.
StateOfLaunches.compilingBody: This report is built from real launch outcomes as founders run their distribution plans through LaunchWake. Once enough launches are tracked (we only publish a channel after at least {min} posts, so no single founder's numbers are ever exposed), the full breakdown appears here — by channel, category, ban risk and timing.
StateOfLaunches.bestConvertersNote: Channels with at least real traffic behind them — raw reach means nothing if it doesn't convert.
StateOfLaunches.methodologyBody: Every number comes from anonymized, aggregate launch outcomes — never any individual account. A channel appears only once it has at least {min} tracked posts behind it, so no single founder's results can be inferred. Channels are grounded in LaunchWake's curated catalog, so the report reflects communities founders actually post in — not invented ones. Conversion is signups ÷ tracked-link clicks; removal rate is removed posts ÷ total posts.
StateOfLaunches.ctaTitle: Want this signal for your next launch?
StateOfLaunches.ctaBody: LaunchWake turns this aggregate into a plan for your specific product: where to post, how to post without getting banned, and which channels actually drive your signups — free, no account needed.
